title: Harden calibration-method default: fall back to sigmoid, not auto/isotonic
source: LdP book audit (6 parallel read-only auditors vs AFML + Machine Learning for Asset Managers), 2026-06-03.
why: fit_calibrated defaults method=auto (train.py:140) -> isotonic when minority>=50; production call sites use cfg[calibration].get(method, auto) (run_xau_d1.py:480,722); build_calibrated_classifier hardcodes isotonic (train.py:126). If calibration.method is dropped from YAML, persisted/live models silently flip to isotonic -> step-function collapse the sigmoid invariant exists to prevent. Inner-CV path falls back to sigmoid correctly (run_xau_d1.py:449); others do not.
value_rationale: Defense-in-depth for a paid-in-blood invariant; one-line fallback removes a config-edit foot-gun.
scope: Make the .get fallback sigmoid at run_xau_d1.py:480,722 (or raise on missing key)
recommendation: Fail-safe to sigmoid everywhere; invariant must not depend on a YAML key being present.
out_of_scope: Changing the chosen calibration method (sigmoid stays correct).

history.reason: LdP book audit: calibration default trap (auto->isotonic)
Implemented: fit_calibrated + build_calibrated_classifier default method -> sigmoid; orchestrator .get fallbacks -> sigmoid. 10 train tests green.
fix: LÃ³pez-de-Prado audit fixes B0129-B0133 (purge, MDA, CFI, DSR, calib)
chore(backlog): provenance for B0129-B0133 LdP-audit-fix commit
test(regression): re-anchor Phase 1 baseline to post-audit-fix numbers (B0129-B0133)
